title: AI Red-Team Loop
enter: Use when an LLM-assisted workflow needs controlled adversarial testing for prompt injection, jailbreak pressure, leakage, or tool misuse.

frames.artifact: Assets, trust boundaries, allowed tools, untrusted inputs, and attacker goals
frames.advance_when: The security boundary and benign task are explicit

frames.artifact: Harmless fixture prompts and canary-bearing test inputs
frames.advance_when: Probes preserve attack shape without operational payloads

frames.artifact: Prompt, transcript, fixture version, and tool-scope record
frames.advance_when: The model output and tool behavior are captured verbatim
Scores cite the transcript and fixture expectations

frames.artifact: Prompt, policy, retrieval, or tool-scope repair
frames.advance_when: The repair addresses the observed failure without blanket refusal

frames.artifact: Repeated run set across fixture versions or model surfaces
frames.advance_when: The failure is fixed or residual risk is accepted explicitly

frames.artifact: Defensive finding with source map, evidence, mitigation, and residual risk
frames.advance_when: A maintainer can reproduce the finding without unsafe payloads

loop.until: Attack resistance and utility preservation meet the release threshold, or residual risk is documented for human review

on_fail: Stop the test if a probe would require operational harmful content; replace it with a defanged skeleton and record the omitted class of detail.
exit: A maintainer can reproduce the finding without unsafe payloads
why_it_works: It treats adversarial promptcraft as evidence-producing security work instead of social-media prompt collecting.
